body:
Type: Bug This started a few days ago in VS Code version 1.110.0. I have updated to VS Code version 1.111.0 and it still happens. It happens when VS Code is started from a shortcut but not when started from the `code.cmd` file or from `Code.exe` on the command line. I use the VS Code integrated terminal running Powershell (Core) to run git commands on the command line and I have VS code set as my git editor (`core.editor=code --wait`). Normally when I run a command like `git commit` or `git rebase --interactive` a file like `COMMIT_EDITMSG` or `git-rebase-todo` will be opened as a new tab in the existing VS Code window. Very recently, when I run those commands, the file is opened in a separate VS Code window and there are error messages printed to the integrated terminal. When running `git commit --all` there will is a short pause, the message `hint: Waiting for your editor to close the file...` is printed to the integrated terminal, and then the `COMMIT_EDITMSG` file is opened in a new VS Code window. After new window opens the following lines are written to the integrated terminal: ``` [46120:0309/104138.853:ERROR:net\disk_cache\cache_util_win.cc:25] Unable to move the cache: Access is denied. (0x5) [46120:0309/104138.853:ERROR:net\disk_cache\disk_cache.cc:236] Unable to create cache [47804:0309/104140.347:ERROR:components\services\storage\service_worker\service_worker_storage.cc:1809] Failed to delete the database: Database IO error ``` The numbers before `:ERROR:` will vary but the parts after `:ERROR:` will always be the same. If I close the new `COMMIT_EDITMSG` window without entering a commit message the `Aborting commit due to empty commit message.` message is written to the integrated terminal. If I do enter a commit message, the commit is created with the specified message. The above describes what happens when I start VS Code from the shortcut in the Windows Start menu's 'All' menu or a shortcut pinned to the taskbar or to the Start Menu. If I start VS code by executing `code` in the Windows Terminal, the `COMMIT_EDITMSG` file opens as a new tab and there are no error messages in the integrated terminal. The `code` command will execute `code.cmd` in `AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\`. If I start VS Code by executing `AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` in the Windows Terminal, the `COMMIT_EDITMSG` file opens as a new tab and there are no error messages in the integrated terminal. I have tried restarting VS Code, restarting my computer, deleting some folders from `C:\Users\xxxxx\AppData\Roaming\Code` (anything with the word cache) and reinstalling VS Code (without first uninstalling) but none of these have worked. title: Agents: "New Session" should remember last harness and isolation mode selection

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
When creating a new session in the Agents window, the isolation mode picker always defaults to "Worktree". It should remember the user's last selection (Worktree vs Folder) and pre-select that option for subsequent new sessions.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open the Agents window
2. Create a new session, selecting "Folder" as the isolation mode
3. Create another new session
4. The isolation mode defaults back to "Worktree" instead of remembering "Folder"

**Expected:** The last-used isolation mode is persisted and pre-selected for new sessions.

title: Terminal tool zsh failures

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
Here's a list of terminal tool failures in my chat sessions from writing bad zsh commands, because the model isn't trained on working in a real shell environment.

Most common ones that I've noticed seeing:
- using `!` incorrectly, eg `echo "hello!"` is an incorrect use of `!`, and this will hang on `dquote>`
- `zsh: command not found: #`
- Incorrect use of `==`, eg:

```
## Request
~~~
id   : toolu_vrtx_01SWtUxMk2tNJW69APCybBo6__vscode-1778605228075
tool : run_in_terminal
args : {
  "command": "cat .planning/config.json 2>/dev/null; echo ===; git log --oneline 4047ede..HEAD | wc -l; git log --format='%ai' 4047ede | tail -1; git log --format='%ai' HEAD | head -1; git diff --stat 4047ede..HEAD | tail -1; find src tests -name '*.ts' | xargs wc -l | tail -1",
  "explanation": "Gather stats",
  "goal": "Stats",
  "mode": "sync"
}
~~~
## Response
~~~
{
  "mode": "yolo",
  "granularity": "coarse",
  "parallelization": true,
  "commit_docs": true,
  "model_profile": "quality",
  "git": {
    "branching_strategy": "phase",
    "phase_branch_template": "phase-{phase}-{slug}"
  },
  "workflow": {
    "research": true,
    "plan_check": true,
    "verifier": true,
    "nyquist_validation": true
  }
}zsh: == not found
```
Full list that were detected in my vscode chat sessions:

| Category                             | Pattern                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Bad substitution                     | JS/template/string snippets in double quotes caused zsh expansion, e.g. `zsh: bad substitution`.                                                                               |
| Comment lines executed               | `# ...` lines were sent to interactive zsh, producing `zsh: command not found: #`; comments with parentheses produced `unknown file attribute` / `invalid mode specification`. |
| Unmatched globs                      | zsh `nomatch` turned missing globs into failures before redirection, e.g. `zsh: no matches found: *.md`.                                                                       |
| Read-only `status` variable          | Commands used `status=$?`; zsh has `status` as a read-only special parameter.                                                                                                  |
| Bare `==` / `===` separators         | `echo ===`, `echo ==== $f`, `echo ===TAIL===` triggered zsh equals expansion, e.g. `zsh: === not found`.                                                                       |
| Multiword command in scalar variable | `DAP="node path/to/file.js"; $DAP ...` failed because zsh does not split scalar variables like bash.                                                                           |
| Missing `timeout`                    | GNU `timeout` assumed on macOS: `zsh: command not found: timeout`.                                                                                                             |
| Bad timeout substitute               | `perl -e 'alarm 3; exec @ARGV' ...` was attempted but produced `zsh: alarm perl ...`.                                                                                          |
| Argument list too long               | Huge JSON/string args sent inline: `zsh: argument list too long: node`.                                                                                                        |

title: Sessions: Back/forward navigation should also navigate between chats within a session

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verification-found
labels.description: Issue verification failed

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

The session back/forward navigation (#315382) currently only tracks session-level transitions. When a session has multiple chats and the user switches between them, the navigation history does not record these chat-level changes. Pressing Back after switching chats within a session does not return to the previous chat.

## Expected Behavior

- Switching between chats within a session should be recorded in the navigation history
- Back/Forward should restore the specific chat that was active at that point in history
- Untitled chats (new-chat-in-session that hasn't been submitted yet) should be excluded from history, consistent with how untitled sessions are handled

## Implementation Notes

- `INavigationEntry` should include an optional `chatResource` alongside `sessionResource`
- The autorun should observe `activeSession.activeChat` in addition to `activeSession`
- `_navigateTo` should call `openChat()` when the entry has a `chatResource`, otherwise `openSession()`
- `_isSameEntry` should compare both session and chat resources

title: Subagent detection in responsesApi.ts is broken (startsWith('subagent') never matches)
body:
## Summary

The subagent gate in `responsesApi.ts` uses `subType?.startsWith('subagent')`, which never matches in practice. Producers set `subType` to `'execution_subagent'` and `'search_subagent'`, so the check evaluates to `false` for every real subagent request.

## Where

`extensions/copilot/src/platform/endpoint/node/responsesApi.ts:69`

```ts
const isAllowedConversationAgent = options.location === ChatLocation.Agent || options.location === ChatLocation.MessagesProxy;
const isSubagent = options.telemetryProperties?.subType?.startsWith('subagent') ?? false;
const shouldDeferTools = toolSearchEnabled && isAllowedConversationAgent && !isSubagent;
```

## Why it's wrong

The producers emit:

| File | Value |
|---|---|
| `extension/prompt/node/executionSubagentToolCallingLoop.ts:366` | `'execution_subagent'` |
| `extension/prompt/node/searchSubagentToolCallingLoop.ts:172` | `'search_subagent'` |

Commit `5e01ed5de06` (*"rename agent type so it doesn't get redacted"*) renamed the values from `subagent/execution` and `subagent/search` to put `subagent` as a suffix rather than a prefix. The detection in `responsesApi.ts` was not updated to match, so `shouldDeferTools` ends up `true` for subagent runs even though the gate is supposed to exclude them.

## Impact

Client-executed tool search defers tools for subagent requests today, contrary to the comment / gate intent. Behavior may or may not be desirable — needs a product call — but at minimum the code does not match the apparent design.

## Suggested fix

```ts
const isSubagent = options.telemetryProperties?.subType?.includes('subagent') ?? false;
```

`includes` matches both current names (`execution_subagent`, `search_subagent`) and stays robust if new subagent types like `explore_subagent` are added later. Same fix was applied to the new extended-cache-ttl gate in `messagesApi.ts` (PR / branch `dev/bhavyau/anthropic-extended-cache-ttl`).

Optionally, extract a tiny shared helper near `subType?: string` in `extensions/copilot/src/platform/networking/common/networking.ts` so both API paths stay in sync.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `responsesApi.ts` correctly identifies `execution_subagent` and `search_subagent` requests as subagents
- [ ] (Optional) Shared helper used by both Messages and Responses API code paths

cc @bhavyaus — found while implementing the Anthropic extended cache TTL gate.

query](https://github.com/issues?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Flsprotocol%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fmonaco-editor%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-anycode%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-autopep8%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-black-formatter%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-copilot%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-copilot-release%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-dev%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-dev-chrome-launcher%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-emmet-helper%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-extension-telemetry%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-flake8%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-github-issue-notebooks%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-hexeditor%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-internalbacklog%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-isort%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-js-debug%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-jupyter%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-jupyter-internal%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-l10n%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-livepreview%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-markdown-languageservice%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-markdown-tm-grammar%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-mypy%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-pull-request-github%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-pylint%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-python%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-python-debugger%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-python-tools-extension-template%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-references-view%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-remote-release%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-remote-repositories-github%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-remote-tunnels%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-remotehub%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-settings-sync-server%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-unpkg%20repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-vsce%20milestone%3A%22June%202025%22%20assignee%3A%40me%20is%3Aissue%20is%3Aclosed%20reason%3Acompleted%20label%3Afeature-request%20-label%3Averification-needed%20-label%3Aon-testplan%20-label%3Averified%20-label%3A*duplicate) vs [running this GitHub Issue Notebook cell](https://insiders.vscode.dev/github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/.vscode/notebooks/my-endgame.github-issues#C9:L1).

GitHub Issue Notebooks loads results:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30efcc36-70e3-4198-b989-011a75c3aae1)

GitHub Issues does not:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1991c863-af18-44e7-abe1-5102f9efd4bb)

From @RileyBroughten

> You'll need to use OR between the repo filters, spaces are treated as AND operators with the introduction of [boolean operators](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/using-issues/filtering-and-searching-issues-and-pull-requests#using-boolean-operators).

So we'll need to update all of our Notebooks before September 4 otherwise they will be broken...

But this is trickier because from within the notebooks I don't think we have a way to tack on this query parameter to adopt this before Sept 4th... so either we need a feature of GHIN that lets you provide this, or we just make the change after they're all broken.

title: Image diff editor fails to render valid PNG files and shows "An error occurred while loading the image"

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
- VS Code Version: 1.110.0
- Commit: 0870c2a0c7c0564e7631bfed2675573a94ba4455
- Date: 2026-03-04T01:20:28.863Z
- OS: macOS 15.7.3
- Architecture: arm64

## Repro steps

1. Start VS Code with a clean user data dir and no extensions.
2. Open a diff between two valid PNG files.

Example repro:

```bash
f="docs/wireframes/mobile/11-mobile-member-delegated-view-request-form.png"
git show HEAD:"$f" > /tmp/vscode-issue-head.png
git show :"$f" > /tmp/vscode-issue-index.png
mkdir -p /tmp/vscode-clean-user /tmp/vscode-clean-ext
open -na "Visual Studio Code" --args \
  --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean-user \
  --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-clean-ext \
  --diff /tmp/vscode-issue-head.png /tmp/vscode-issue-index.png
```

## Expected result

VS Code should render both PNG files in the image diff editor.

## Actual result

Both sides of the diff show:

`An error occurred while loading the image.`

In Source Control this also prevents comparing PNG changes from Git.

## Additional notes

This does not appear to be a repository or Git problem:

- The issue reproduces with two real files in `/tmp`, not only with `git:` resources.
- It reproduces with a clean `--user-data-dir` and empty `--extensions-dir`.
- It reproduces after resetting VS Code workspace state and cache.

The PNGs used in the repro are valid:

```bash
file /tmp/vscode-issue-head.png
# PNG image data, 350 x 760, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced

file /tmp/vscode-issue-index.png
# PNG image data, 390 x 844, 8-bit/color RGB, non-interlaced
```

## Logs

`renderer.log` only reports a generic error:

```text
2026-03-06 15:23:34.779 [error] An unknown error occurred. Please consult the log for more details.
```

I can provide sample PNGs if needed.

title: Per-turn agent filtering busts the system prompt cache

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Symptom

Across two consecutive chat requests in the same session, the entire ~9.7k-token system block is byte-identical except for a single 9-character substring inside the `<agents>` listing:

```diff
@@ -544,3 +544,3 @@
 <agent>
-<name>Plan</name>
+<name>Ask</name>
 </agent>
```

The system-level prompt cache breakpoint sits at the end of the system block, so this single-line mutation invalidates the entire system prefix. The model re-pays the cost of caching ~9.7k tokens of otherwise-stable instructions on every turn that flips the active mode.

title: splice(indexOf()) without -1 guard silently removes wrong array element in 4 locations

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.120.0 (main branch, commit 89fc6394f59)
- OS Version: All platforms

## Bug

Multiple locations in the codebase use the pattern `array.splice(array.indexOf(item), 1)` without checking whether `indexOf` returned `-1`. When the element is not found, `indexOf` returns `-1`, and `splice(-1, 1)` silently removes the **last** element of the array instead of being a no-op.

This causes incorrect behavior: the wrong keyboard layout, activity badge, progress indicator, or settings entry is silently removed.

## Affected locations

| File | Method | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| `keyboardLayoutService.ts:116-119` | `removeKeyboardLayout` | Removes wrong layout from MRU if called with stale ref |
| `activityService.ts:95, 158` | `showViewContainerActivity` / `showActivity` dispose callback | Removes wrong activity badge on double dispose |
| `progressService.ts:135` | `withWindowProgress` finally block | Removes wrong progress task if promise resolved before delay |
| `settingsTree.ts:2871` | `ApplySettingToAllProfilesAction.run` | Removes wrong setting from all-profiles list (TOCTOU between `this.checked` and fresh `getValue()`) |

## Steps to Reproduce

**`activityService.ts` (double dispose scenario):**
1. Register a view container activity via `showViewContainerActivity`
2. Store the returned `IDisposable`
3. Call `.dispose()` on it twice
4. On the second call, `indexOf` returns `-1` because the activity was already removed
5. `splice(-1, 1)` removes the last activity in the array (belonging to a different view container)
6. The wrong activity badge disappears from the UI

**`settingsTree.ts` (TOCTOU scenario):**
1. Open Settings UI
2. Toggle "Apply Setting to All Profiles" for a setting
3. If another VS Code window or process removes that setting from the `configurationService.allProfiles` list between the `this.checked` guard (set via config listener) and the `getValue()` call, `indexOf` returns `-1`
4. `splice(-1, 1)` removes the last entry in the all-profiles list (a different setting)

## Fix

A PR is available: #314635

The fix adds `index !== -1` guards before each `splice` call. This is a standard defensive pattern with zero cost.

title: Listener leak in Getting Started page: onDidChangeConfiguration registered inside onDidProgressStep callback

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.120.0 (main branch, commit 89fc6394f59)
- OS Version: All platforms

## Bug

In `GettingStartedPage` (the Welcome/Walkthrough editor), a `configurationService.onDidChangeConfiguration` listener for `REDUCED_MOTION_KEY` is registered inside the `onDidProgressStep` event callback using `this._register()`. Each time a walkthrough step is completed, a new permanent configuration listener accumulates in the disposable store and is never cleaned up.

After completing N walkthrough steps across any walkthroughs, N identical `onDidChangeConfiguration` listeners exist, each toggling the same CSS class on every config change. This grows without bound for the lifetime of the editor.

This is the exact anti-pattern the project's own [coding guidelines](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/.github/copilot-instructions.md) warn about:

> Do NOT register a disposable to the containing class if the object is created within a method that is called repeatedly to avoid leaks. Instead, return an IDisposable from such method and let the caller register it.

## Affected code

**File:** `src/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/browser/gettingStarted.ts:275`

```typescript
this._register(this.gettingStartedService.onDidProgressStep(step => {
    // ... step handling ...

    // BUG: registered inside event callback, accumulates on every step completion
    this._register(this.configurationService.onDidChangeConfiguration(e => {
        if (e.affectsConfiguration(REDUCED_MOTION_KEY)) {
            this.container.classList.toggle('animatable', this.shouldAnimate());
        }
    }));

    ourStep.done = step.done;
    // ...
}));
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the Welcome tab (Getting Started page)
2. Complete several walkthrough steps (e.g., toggle "Choose your Theme", "Rich Language Support", etc.)
3. Each step completion fires `onDidProgressStep`, which registers a new `onDidChangeConfiguration` listener
4. After completing 10 steps, there are 10 identical config listeners in the disposable store
5. These listeners remain until the Getting Started editor is closed
6. Reopening the Getting Started editor and completing more steps adds more listeners

## Impact

- Memory grows linearly with walkthrough step completions
- Each redundant listener runs on every configuration change event, wasting CPU
- Users who explore multiple walkthroughs accumulate dozens of dead listeners

## Fix

A PR is available: #314636

The fix moves the `onDidChangeConfiguration` subscription to the constructor, alongside the other config/event listeners, so it is registered exactly once.

<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.118.1
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 24.6.0

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Begin a chat session using a model selected from a BYOK provider or an extension providing a [Language Model Chat Provider](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/ai/language-model-chat-provider)
2. Use enough turns to cause the model to think and/or call tools.
3. Invoke the `/compact` slash command directly via chat
4. The request that is sent to the upstream server contains no "assistant" role blocks.
5. The session becomes basically unusable after the compaction completes due to the missing critical context from the summary.

The "assistant" turns seem to be filtered out of the request because there is no tool config being passed into the prompt formatting code in these two spots:
1. https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/intents/node/agentIntent.ts#L272
2. https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/intents/node/agentIntent.ts#L282

The following code snippet produces a working build that I am then able to use to compact my conversations correctly:
```typescript
...
		const availableTools = await this.instantiationService.invokeFunction(getAgentTools, request, endpoint);
		const { toolReferences, toolInvocationToken } = request;
		const promptContext: IBuildPromptContext = {
			history,
			chatVariables: new ChatVariablesCollection([]),
			query: '',
			toolCallRounds: [],
			conversation,
			tools: {
				availableTools,
				toolReferences: toolReferences.map((item) => InternalToolReference.from(item)),
				toolInvocationToken
			}
		};

		try {
			const propsBuilder = this.instantiationService.createInstance(SummarizedConversationHistoryPropsBuilder);
			const propsInfo = propsBuilder.getProps({
				priority: 1,
				endpoint,
				location: ChatLocation.Agent,
				promptContext,
				tools: availableTools,
				maxToolResultLength: Infinity,
			});
...
```

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.86.2
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 23.3.0


![Screen Recording 2024-02-23 at 22 23 44](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/26697004/71f56ae1-7773-4ac4-8a1e-85aae8670662)

As described [in this issue](https://github.com/yatki/vscode-surround/issues/108), I'm calling `activeEditor.insertSnippet` method to insert a snippet. `insertSnippet` method **is** inserting the snippet for each selection, but it is **not** detecting the related selection's content.


Steps to Reproduce:

1. In an empty editor, create a few lines.
2. Create a cursor at the end of every line with [option] + click
3. Select the lines with [shift] + [left arrow]
4. Run the code below:
```ts
    const snippetStr = "if(${1:condition}) {\n\t$TM_SELECTED_TEXT\n}$0"
    await activeEditor.insertSnippet(new SnippetString(snippetStr));
```

Output: 
- Every line will be wrapped with `if` statement but the $TM_SELECTED_TEXT **only** returns the text of **the last** selected block.

Expected:
- `activeEditor.insertSnippet` method should use the correct selection's content.

body:

<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.47.0
- VS Code Version: 1.119.0
- OS Version: Linux x64 6.19.14-200.fc43.x86_64
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Sandbox
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Any

Steps to Reproduce:

1. As example I asked it to check that redis is running on localhost
2. It tries to run terminal command in sandbox but it ends up:
    - opening another vscode window with cli.js

```
ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 cd '/home/pslama/.vscode/tmp/tmp_vscode_1';  PATH="$PATH:/usr/share/code/resources/app/node_modules/@vscode/ripgrep/bin" TMPDIR="/home/pslama/.vscode/tmp/tmp_vscode_1" CLAUDE_TMPDIR="/home/pslama/.vscode/tmp/tmp_vscode_1" "/usr/share/code/code" "/usr/share/code/re
sources/app/node_modules/@vscode/sandbox-runtime/dist/cli.js" --settings "/home/pslama/.vscode/tmp/tmp_vscode_1/vscode-sandbox
-settings-80d4e1e1-c51d-40fa-9f50-8426e1bf5441.json" -c 'cd '\''/home/pslama/wrkspaces/__redacted_code_directory__'\'' && di
renv exec /home/pslama/wrkspaces/__redacted_code_directory__ sh -lc '\''if command -v nc >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "*1\r\
n$4\r\nPING\r\n" | nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 6379; else echo "nc not available"; fi'\'''
```

title: sessions: move IAgentHostFilterService out of providers layer

labels.name: debt
labels.description: Code quality issues

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Architecture context

The `src/vs/sessions/contrib/` folder is organized into two areas:

- **`contrib/providers/`** — Session provider implementations (e.g. `copilotChatSessions`, `remoteAgentHost`, `agentHost`). These are isolated backend implementations that should not be imported by other contrib folders.
- **`contrib/<feature>/`** — Feature contributions (e.g. `chat`, `changes`, `sessions`). These implement UI features and may depend on services but must **not** import directly from `contrib/providers/`.

The dependency direction is **providers → sessions workbench**, not the other way around. Providers depend on the sessions workbench services and APIs to register themselves and contribute UI. The sessions workbench must never depend on provider internals.

An ESLint layer rule (`contrib/providers/*/~` before `contrib/*/~` in `eslint.config.js`) enforces this boundary. See `src/vs/sessions/LAYERS.md` for the full layering rules.

## Problem

`IAgentHostFilterService` is defined in `contrib/providers/remoteAgentHost/` but consumed by three non-provider feature contributions, violating the layer rule. Each import currently has an `eslint-disable-next-line` suppression:

| File | Line |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/newChatViewPane.ts` | 33 |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/webWorkspacePicker.ts` | 23 |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/sessions/browser/views/sessionsList.ts` | 48 |

## Guidance

Provider-specific information — including anything from agent host — must not leak into the sessions workbench layer. All information should flow through the session provider interfaces (`ISessionsProvider`, `ISessionsManagementService`) or via menu/command contributions. If a feature contribution needs data that currently lives in a provider, the provider should expose it through the existing session provider contract or contribute UI via menus, not through direct imports.

**Important:** Do not add provider-specific concepts to `ISessionsProvider` or `ISessionsManagementService` to work around this. Only extend those interfaces with functionality that is genuinely generic and applicable to all providers.

title: BYOK Responses retry for previous_response_not_found re-sends previous_response_id because OpenAIEndpoint overwrites ignoreStatefulMarker

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

body:
Type: Bug ### Environment - VS Code Insiders - Built-in Copilot extension bundled with Insiders - BYOK OpenAI-compatible endpoint - Responses API (Codex CLI Backend) - Reproduced against a local OpenAI-compatible test server ### Summary When a BYOK Responses request fails with HTTP 400: ```json { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx.", "error": { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx." } } ``` VS Code appears to retry, but the retry still includes the same `previous_response_id`. Expected behavior is to retry once **without** the stateful marker / `previous_response_id`. ### Expected behavior After receiving HTTP 400 with root-level: - `code: "previous_response_not_found"` - `message: "No response found for previous_response_id ..."` the client should classify the failure as an invalid stateful marker and retry once without `previous_response_id`. ### Actual behavior A second `POST /v1/responses` is sent, but it still contains the same `previous_response_id`, so the request fails again with the same 400 and the user sees an error instead of recovery. ### Repro 1. Configure Copilot BYOK against an OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoint. 2. Start a stateful conversation so the client sends `previous_response_id`. 3. Make the server return HTTP 400 with: ```json { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx.", "error": { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx." } } ``` 4. Observe that VS Code sends a second request. 5. Observe that the second request still contains the same `previous_response_id`. ### Notes I first suspected the server payload shape, but I verified the live server already returns root-level `code` and `message` as required. The remaining issue is that the retry request still includes the original `previous_response_id`. ### Evidence The request log shows: - first `POST /v1/responses` with `previous_response_id = X` - HTTP 400 - second `POST /v1/responses` also with `previous_response_id = X` - HTTP 400 again So the retry is happening, but the stateful marker is not being cleared. ### Code analysis The retry path appears to be intended to work: - `extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompt/node/chatMLFetcher.ts` - recognizes HTTP 400 with `jsonData.code === 'previous_response_not_found'` - `extensions/copilot/src/platform/endpoint/node/chatEndpoint.ts` - converts that into `InvalidStatefulMarker` - retries with `ignoreStatefulMarker: true` However, the BYOK `OpenAIEndpoint` appears to override that retry flag. #### 1) `createRequestBody()` mutates `ignoreStatefulMarker` `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts` ```ts const zdr = !!this.modelMetadata.zeroDataRetentionEnabled; options.ignoreStatefulMarker = zdr; const body = super.createRequestBody(options); ``` This overwrites `ignoreStatefulMarker: true` during retry back to `false` in the normal non-ZDR case. #### 2) `makeChatRequest2()` also forces `ignoreStatefulMarker: false` `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts` ```ts const modifiedOptions: IMakeChatRequestOptions = { ...options, ignoreStatefulMarker: false }; const response = await super.makeChatRequest2(modifiedOptions, token); ``` Even if this is not the primary reason for the observed retry failure, it is also suspicious because it prevents upstream intent from being preserved. ### Proposed patch I believe the BYOK endpoint should preserve an existing retry flag instead of overwriting it. ```diff diff --git a/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts b/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts index XXXXXXX..YYYYYYY 100644 --- a/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts +++ b/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts @@ -240,7 +240,7 @@ export class OpenAIEndpoint extends ChatEndpoint { if (this.useResponsesApi) { // Handle Responses API: customize the body directly const zdr = !!this.modelMetadata.zeroDataRetentionEnabled; - options.ignoreStatefulMarker = zdr; + options.ignoreStatefulMarker = !!options.ignoreStatefulMarker || zdr; const body = super.createRequestBody(options); body.store = !zdr; body.n = undefined; @@ -367,10 +367,8 @@ export class OpenAIEndpoint extends ChatEndpoint { } public override async makeChatRequest2(options: IMakeChatRequestOptions, token: CancellationToken): Promise { - // Apply ignoreStatefulMarker: false for initial request - const modifiedOptions: IMakeChatRequestOptions = { ...options, ignoreStatefulMarker: false }; - const response = await super.makeChatRequest2(modifiedOptions, token); - return hydrateBYOKErrorMessages(response); + const response = await super.makeChatRequest2(options, token); + return hydrateBYOKErrorMessages(response); } } ``` ### Why this patch - `ChatEndpoint.makeChatRequest2()` already computes the default behavior for `ignoreStatefulMarker`. - A retry from `InvalidStatefulMarker` should be allowed to preserve `ignoreStatefulMarker: true`. - ZDR should still disable stateful marker reuse, but it should not erase an already-true retry flag. ### Suggested regression tests Please consider adding a BYOK regression test covering this flow: 1. initial Responses request uses a stateful marker 2. server returns HTTP 400 `previous_response_not_found` 3. retry is triggered 4. retry request body omits `previous_response_id` A targeted test around `OpenAIEndpoint.createRequestBody()` preserving `ignoreStatefulMarker: true` would also help. VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.121.0-insider (f805d63defafb2ae798360714d6e7600d154a6df, 2026-05-15T12:52:51-04:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Reduce context size of add element to chat

body:
Currently it includes all computed styles, all applicable CSS rules, and all variables. But that's a ton of context and token use.

The attachment should be more focused and just include computed styles that have been modified, and CSS rules and variables that are actually affecting the element.

If the context is reduced enough, we could probably disable the `attachCss` setting as well and just always include it.

title: Allow sending chat requests with attachments only

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

body: When using chat, attaching an image or file currently requires typing text before the request can be sent. This makes attachment-only prompts awkward, especially for screenshots or files where the attachment itself is the complete request context. Expected behavior: - Users can send a chat request with only explicit attachments, such as images, files, etc. - The UI should not render an empty-looking request. - For attachment-only requests, the user-visible request text should be a localized summary such as: - `Attached 1 image` - `Attached 2 images` - `Attached 1 file` - `Attached 2 files` - Or find a better way to do that Attached photo: attachment selected, but send is blocked until text is typed.

title: read_file cannot read vscode-chat-response-resource:// URIs returned by mcp_github_get_file_contents

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body: Type: Bug When mcp_github_get_file_contents downloads a file, it wraps the content in a vscode-chat-response-resource:// URI and includes a hint saying: `Hint: you can read the full contents of any truncated resources by passing their URIs as the absolutePath to the read_file.` However, passing that URI to read_file consistently fails with EntryNotFound (FileSystemError): File not found. The resource is inaccessible immediately after being returned by the tool. **Steps to reproduce** In a Copilot agent/chat session, call mcp_github_get_file_contents on any file in a GitHub repository. Observe that the response contains a vscode-chat-response-resource://... URI with the note resource not found - the file may have been deleted or become inaccessible. Pass that URI as absolutePath to read_file as the hint suggests. Observe the error. **Expected behavior** read_file successfully reads the downloaded content via the resource URI, as the tool hint implies. **Actual behavior** The tool also says resource not found - the file may have been deleted or become inaccessible inline in the response — even though the tool simultaneously reports successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 11f713054004ab286124ff17d8157866635873ab). **Additional observations** Affects both the main Copilot chat agent and agents running as subagents. The file SHA and metadata are correct — the GitHub API download succeeds; the failure is in the resource handoff to read_file. VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.120.0-insider (18a02327e146205cf696f8a5babbd036b6a8f743, 2026-05-06T04:51:16Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22631 Modes:

title: BYOK providers tool calling broken in v1.120 — Unknown tokenizer: undefined

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: chat-ext-issue
labels.description: Triaging assistance tag. Automatically applied if an issue comes from the chat oss repo.

body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Latest (as of May 2026)
- VS Code Version: 1.120 (commit 79d6cea)
- OS Version: Windows / Linux / macOS
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent Mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Any BYOK provider (Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible, Ollama)
- Logs: Error thrown — `Unknown tokenizer: undefined`

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure a BYOK provider in GitHub Copilot Chat (Anthropic / OpenAI-compatible endpoint / Ollama)
2. Switch to Agent Mode in Copilot Chat
3. Send a prompt that triggers a tool call (e.g. file read, code edit)
4. Observe the error: `Unknown tokenizer: undefined` is thrown directly back to the LLM, breaking the agentic workflow

**Expected behavior:**
Tool calls via BYOK providers should work correctly, as they did in VS Code 1.119 and prior.

**Actual behavior:**
Agent Mode fails with `Unknown tokenizer: undefined`. Copilot cannot resolve the tokenizer for external/custom models, breaking all tool-calling steps entirely.

**Workaround:**
Downgrading to VS Code 1.119 resolves the issue.

**Related Issues:**
- #314419 — Unknown tokenizer error in Copilot Agent Mode
- #314399 — 1.120 broke BYOK providers tool calling

title: Terminal follow-up with sandboxing is too verbose
body: Testing #313618 With terminal sandboxing, the command is way too verbose. Actual command here is just "sleep 5". I think we should probably keep track of the original command before sandboxing and display that instead.

title: BulkTextEdit applies edit although text document version has advanced.

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
For a video showing this see https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-languageserver-node/issues/1756

Although the workspace edit is created using an LSP server the actual problem that this happens is in `BulkTextEdit#apply` (see https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/b8b5144cc6d926aa50402ca2511f3068a6bc7c54/src/vs/workbench/contrib/bulkEdit/browser/bulkTextEdits.ts#L293). 

The method itself is async. It first validates the edit for correct version and the asynchronously creates the edit task, which gives the editor the possibility to accept and process keystrokes.

Easy fix would IMO be to two the two statements so that the code looks like this

```
		const tasks = await this._createEditsTasks();
		this._validateBeforePrepare();
```

title: Integrated terminal exits immediately in Insiders 43d0ed on macOS arm64
body:
Type: Bug

VS Code Insiders: `1.119.0-insider`
Commit: `43d0ed45492da1b1ca1738893956de429d46b5e8`
OS: macOS arm64

Steps:
1. Open Insiders with clean user data and no user extensions.
2. Run `Terminal: Create New Terminal`.
3. Also reproduced with `createTerminal()` using `/bin/sh -c 'sleep 300'`.

Actual:
Terminal opens, then closes immediately. Shell command never starts.

Pty host trace:
- `_onProcessReady` fires.
- `TerminalProcess#_queueProcessExit` fires about 2 ms later.
- `node-pty.IPty#resize exception ioctl(2) failed, EBADF`.
- Exit event: `0`.

Expected:
Terminal stays open and shell starts.

Note:
Does not reproduce in later Insiders commit `7e4091cc0c2607b3445c2c05b74b940e68e61413`.

body:
Type: Bug 1. Open a new, untitled Markdown tab (for me, Markdown is the default language) 2. Write some markdown, ideally including some unique string you can search for later 3. Without saving the untitled tab, invoke the `Markdown: Toggle Preview View` action (via the Command Palette, or in my case via `Ctrl+Shift+V`, a keyboard shortcut I use without thinking all the time) 4. Observe an empty markdown preview 5. Observe that your source markdown is gone forever - no trace in the History or Backup folders https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90e2ca06-6277-48b6-873e-8b623a392b53 I can't say exactly when I last used `Ctrl+Shift+V` on an untitled/unsaved file successfully and unfortunately I didn't notice the problem today until after I installed the latest update. It's possible the keyboard shortcut was assigned to a different command before this update but I don't remember for sure. For now I'm going to work around this issue by reassigning the shortcut to `Markdown: Open Preview` which appears to be working fine. This problem was reported back in 2020. 

Stefan wrote a very good summary here:

Nobody answered and this is still a problem ⚠️.
The person @joaomoreno that thinks the current behavior is correct has also never responded in #96904 to why he wanted this behavior.

Steps to Reproduce:

3.  Hit Command-Shift-B to build without running -> dirty files are auto-saved, but not formatted, and no code actions are run. This is not 

Please reconsider simply merging #104298

title: runInTerminal tool: AI models use short timeouts for long-running commands, causing premature termination
body:
## Problem

When AI agents use the `runInTerminal` tool, they often set short timeouts (e.g. 30 000 ms) for commands that inherently take a long time — such as `npm install`, `cargo build`, `docker build`, or `qemu-system-*`. This causes the tool to return before the command finishes, leading to false failures or incomplete results.

The existing tool description told models to "set a generous timeout as a safety net", but models would still pick timeouts that are far too short.

## Root Cause

1. The model description encouraged setting *some* timeout but did not clearly say to omit it for one-shot long-running commands.
2. There was no server-side enforcement: even if a model passed a 5-second timeout for `npm install`, it was used as-is.

## Fix

- **Update model descriptions** in `runInTerminalTool.ts` to instruct models to **omit timeout by default** for one-shot long-running commands (builds, compiles, package installs, downloads, test suites, VM boots), and only set one when a safety cap is genuinely needed. Forbid short timeouts (<60 000 ms) for those categories.
- **Add `TIMEOUT_RULES`** — a table of regex patterns paired with minimum timeout durations covering common long-running command categories (npm/pip/brew installs, cargo/make builds, docker, qemu, git clone, test runners).
- **Enforce minimum timeouts** at invocation time: when the model supplies a timeout that is below the known minimum for a matched pattern, silently raise it to the minimum and log a debug message.

## Impact

AI agents running package installs, builds, or VM operations no longer get cut off by inappropriately short timeouts.

title: Models showing from BYOK OpenRouter even after removing that configuration

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

WSL Ubuntu 24.04 environment.
Version: 1.118.0-insider (user setup)
Commit: 46e64ce993dba8618aaa24e7194639fc92147d7f
Date: 2026-04-23T08:49:02+02:00
Electron: 39.8.8
ElectronBuildId: 13870025
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: latest?
- VS Code Version:  
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Model selector
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): All
- Logs:


Issue Summary

OpenRouter BYOK-related models continue to appear in the VS Code model picker even after I tried to remove them, and I do not use Settings Sync, so this does not appear to be coming from synced settings.

What I checked

I inspected the normal local user configuration on Windows and did not find any active OpenRouter configuration:

[chatLanguageModels.json](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/DEXTER/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code%20Insiders/46e64ce993/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) is empty.
[settings.json](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/DEXTER/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code%20Insiders/46e64ce993/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) does not contain OpenRouter, BYOK, or Copilot gateway settings.
The main VS Code user storage files and state database did not contain any openrouter or byok entries.
I then searched the broader VS Code Insiders user-data area. The only matches I found were in chat/session history and logs, which show that OpenRouter had been used previously. I did not find an obvious live config entry that explains why those models still appear in the picker.

Expected Behavior

If OpenRouter BYOK models are no longer configured, they should disappear from the model picker.

Actual Behavior

The OpenRouter-related models still appear in the model picker even though:

Settings Sync is disabled
local model config is empty
local user settings do not contain OpenRouter/BYOK config
no active OpenRouter/BYOK entries were found in standard local storage
Likely Cause

This looks like stale model/provider state being cached somewhere outside the normal user settings flow, possibly in extension state or remote/WSL-side VS Code server state, rather than in local Windows settings.

Why this seems like a VS Code bug

From a user perspective, there is no discoverable place to remove these model entries once they have been introduced. If the picker is backed by cached provider state, that state either:

is not being cleared when config is removed, or
is being loaded from a hidden location that is not surfaced to the user

title: VS Code Server web: GitHub auth tokens lost on page refresh/folder switch (in-memory only)
body:
## Description

When using VS Code Server (`server-linux-x64-web`) accessed via a browser, GitHub authentication tokens (Copilot, git operations) are stored **in-memory only** and lost whenever the page is refreshed or a different folder is opened.

## Root Cause

In `src/vs/code/browser/workbench/workbench.ts` (line 619-621), the `secretStorageProvider` is set to `undefined` when `remoteAuthority` is set and the `vscode-secret-key-path` cookie is missing:

```typescript
secretStorageProvider: config.remoteAuthority && !secretStorageKeyPath
    ? undefined /* with a remote without embedder-preferred storage, store on the remote */
    : new LocalStorageSecretStorageProvider(secretStorageCrypto),
```

The `vscode-secret-key-path` cookie is only set by the CLI (`cli/src/commands/serve_web.rs`), **not** by the server (`src/vs/server/node/webClientServer.ts`). So when using the server binary directly, the cookie is never set → `secretStorageProvider` is `undefined` → `BrowserSecretStorageService` falls back to `super(true, ...)` → **in-memory only**.

## Impact

- Copilot requires re-authentication after every page refresh or folder switch
- GitHub authentication for git operations is lost
- Any extension using `SecretStorage` API loses its data

## Proposed Fix

Always use `LocalStorageSecretStorageProvider` which persists encrypted secrets in VS Code Server web context stores encrypted secrets in the browser's `localStorage`:

```typescript
secretStorageProvider: new LocalStorageSecretStorageProvider(secretStorageCrypto),
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start VS Code Server: `code-server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8443`
2. Open in browser, sign in to GitHub via VS Code accounts menu
3. Copilot works
4. Refresh the page or open a different folder
5. Copilot prompts for re-authentication

## Environment

- VS Code Server `server-linux-x64-web` (latest stable)
- Browser: any (Chrome, Firefox, Edge)
- OS: Linux

title: Chronicle cloud sync: circuit breaker never recovers within a session

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

The chronicle cloud sync circuit breaker transitions to an open (tripped) state after repeated failures, but never transitions back to a half-open or closed state within the same VS Code session.

Once the breaker trips, cloud sync is effectively dead until the user restarts VS Code. There is no periodic retry or half-open probe to detect when the transient issue has resolved.

## Expected behavior

The circuit breaker should implement a standard half-open state:
- After a cooldown period (e.g., 5 minutes), attempt a single probe request
- If the probe succeeds, transition back to closed and resume normal sync
- If the probe fails, remain open and schedule the next probe with exponential backoff

## Current behavior

- Circuit breaker opens after repeated failures (observed: ~17 consecutive failures before trip)
- Stays permanently open for the remainder of the session
- User must restart VS Code to get cloud sync working again
- No telemetry or notification informs the user that sync has stopped

## Impact

Users in long-running sessions (common for remote/SSH developers) lose cloud sync for hours or days without realizing it. Their session history stops syncing silently.

## Suggested fix

1. Add a half-open state with periodic retry (e.g., exponential backoff starting at 5min, capped at 30min)
2. Emit telemetry when the breaker recovers (closed transition) to validate the fix
3. Consider a status bar indicator or notification when sync is degraded

title: Chronicle cloud sync: createCloudSession should gracefully handle policy-blocked users

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

When enterprise policies block cloud session creation, the chronicle cloud sync repeatedly attempts to create sessions and fails. There is no caching of the policy-blocked state, so the same request is retried on every session start, generating unnecessary failures and eventually tripping the circuit breaker.

## Current behavior

1. User starts a chat session
2. Chronicle attempts createCloudSession
3. Server returns a policy-blocked error
4. Next session start: same thing repeats
5. After enough failures, circuit breaker trips (see #317330)
6. All cloud sync stops — including for users who might have mixed repos (some allowed, some not)

## Expected behavior

1. On first policy_blocked response, cache the result with a TTL (e.g., 1 hour or until next window reload)
2. Skip createCloudSession attempts for that workspace/org during the TTL
3. Do not count policy_blocked as a circuit breaker failure (it is an expected policy response, not a transient error)
4. Optionally surface a one-time notification: cloud sync is not available for this workspace due to organization policy

## Impact

As chronicle rolls out more broadly, enterprise users with restrictive policies will see repeated silent failures. The current design conflates policy enforcement with infrastructure errors, causing the circuit breaker to trip unnecessarily.

## Suggested fix

1. Distinguish policy_blocked from transient errors in the response handling
2. Cache the blocked state per-workspace with TTL
3. Exclude policy responses from the circuit breaker failure counter
4. Add a pre-flight check or early-exit before attempting createCloudSession when cached as blocked

labels.name: verified

labels.description: VS Code installation and upgrade system issues

body:
>!-- bug_report_template -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- - OS Version: Windows 11
- - - PowerShell Version: 5.1.26100.8115
## Description

When opening VS Code using a custom PowerShell script (.ps1), two instances of VS Code are launched instead of one. No error messages are displayed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a PowerShell script (.ps1) that opens VS Code (e.g., using `code .` or `Start-Process code`)
2. 2. Execute the .ps1 script from a PowerShell terminal
3. 3. Observe that two separate VS Code windows open instead of the expected single instance
## Expected Behavior

Running the .ps1 script should open exactly one instance of VS Code.

## Actual Behavior

Two instances of VS Code are opened. No error messages are shown in the terminal or in VS Code.

## Environment

- VS Code: 1.116.0
- - OS: Windows 11
- - - PowerShell: 5.1 (Build 26100.8115)

title: Missing separator after locale in Copilot title prompt causes malformed prompt text

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-prompts
labels.description: Prompt and Instruction files related issues

body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version:1.119.0 (user setup) - 8b640eef5a6c6089c029249d48efa5c99adf7d51
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): N/A
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): N/A
- Logs:

<details><summary>Logs</summary>

```json
[
    {
        "role": "system",
        "parts": [
            {
                "type": "text",
                "content": "You are an expert in crafting ultra-compact titles for chatbot conversations. You are presented with a chat request, and you reply with only a brief title that captures the main topic of that request.\nFollow Microsoft content policies.\nAvoid content that violates copyrights.\nIf you are asked to generate content that is harmful, hateful, racist, sexist, lewd, or violent, only respond with \"Sorry, I can't assist with that.\"\nKeep your answers short and impersonal.\nRespond in the following locale: zh-CNWrite the title in sentence case, not title case. Preserve product names, abbreviations, code symbols, and proper nouns.\nAim for 3-6 words. Prefer the shortest accurate title.\nDrop articles like \"a\", \"an\", and \"the\" unless needed for clarity.\nDrop filler and generic framing like \"help with\", \"question about\", \"request for\", or \"issue with\".\nPrefer short, concrete synonyms and omit unnecessary words.\nDo not wrap the title in quotes or add trailing punctuation.\nHere are some examples of good titles:\n- Git rebase question\n- Install Python packages\n- LinkedList implementation location\n- Add VS Code tree view\n- React useState usage"
            }
        ]
    },
    {
        "role": "user",
        "parts": [
            {
                "type": "text",
                "content": "Please write a brief title for the following request:\n\n请你分析该脚本有何优化空间"
            }
        ]
    }
]
```

</details>

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Setup OTEL for copilot 
2. Start a conversation
3. Analyze the log.

----

In the Copilot extension, there is a prompt used to generate a short title based on the conversation content:

The prompt includes a localized instruction inserted via `ResponseTranslationRules`:

<https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/2e7c5fdfbb6270e124d884012ddb36357a77b2e5/extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompts/node/panel/title.tsx#L14-L22>

Which comes from here:

<https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/2e7c5fdfbb6270e124d884012ddb36357a77b2e5/extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompts/node/base/responseTranslationRules.tsx#L48-L51>

However, in `responseTranslationRules.tsx`, the generated locale line does not end with a period or any other separator. As a result, the following text is concatenated directly with the locale value:

```text
[TRUNCATED]
Respond in the following locale: zh-CNWrite the title in sentence case, not title case. Preserve product names, abbreviations, code symbols, and proper nouns.
[TRUNCATED]
```

As shown above, `zh-CN` is directly merged with `Write`, which makes the final prompt malformed.

It would be great to fix this by adding a separator after the locale value so the prompt is generated correctly.

I would be happy to submit a PR to fix this.

title: Webview extensions blank on Safari/WebKit (vscode.dev / code-server / code serve-web): VSBuffer.slice returns view that gets detached by extension-host postMessage transfer

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: safari
labels.description: Issues running VSCode in Web on Safari

labels.name: vscode.dev
labels.description: Issues related to vscode.dev

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

### VS Code Version

Reproduced against code-server `4.106.x` (vendoring `vscode` `1.107.x`). Bug exists in current `microsoft/vscode@main` per source inspection of `src/vs/base/common/buffer.ts` and `src/vs/base/parts/ipc/common/ipc.net.ts`. Per the introducing commit (microsoft/vscode#76076) it has been present since 2019.

### OS Version

- **Server**: Ubuntu 24.04, Node 20.x
- **Clients (reproduces)**: Safari 18 on macOS 15, Safari 18 on iPadOS 18, Chrome / Brave / Firefox on iOS (all forced WebKit by App Store policy)
- **Clients (does not reproduce)**: Chrome / Chromium / Brave / Edge on macOS / Linux / Windows (V8), Firefox desktop (SpiderMonkey)

### Does this reproduce in native VS Code desktop?

**No.** Native VS Code (Electron) uses Electron's IPC layer between renderer and extension host — not browser `postMessage` with transfer lists for `VSBuffer` payloads. The underlying `ArrayBuffer` is never detached in that flow, so `ChunkStream`'s held subarray views stay valid and `VSBuffer.slice`'s view-returning behavior is correct. The bug is specifically a browser-mode VS Code issue.

### Does this reproduce in `code serve-web`?

**Yes (architecturally identical, not directly stood up).** `code serve-web` ships the same `VSBuffer.slice` code path and the same browser-based web-worker extension host. Any Safari / iOS client of `code serve-web` should hit it identically. Reproduces deterministically in `code-server`, which vendors the same upstream code verbatim.

### Does this reproduce in `vscode.dev`?

**Likely yes.** I have not directly tested in Safari since `vscode.dev` is harder to drive a webview-heavy extension on (the marketplace surface is narrower). Long-standing issue #213143 ("Safari 14 no longer able to load vscode.dev") and adjacent reports point in the same direction. Same upstream code path.

### Does this reproduce in GitHub Codespaces?

**Likely yes** (Codespaces uses VS Code web). Not directly tested.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run any browser-mode VS Code (vscode.dev, `code serve-web`, code-server, Codespaces) over HTTPS — service workers must register.
2. Activate any extension that delivers initial webview content as multi-chunk binary IPC frames. Reliable repro: Claude Code (`Anthropic.claude-code` version `2.1.77` specifically; newer versions hit an unrelated Anthropic-side regression that masks this bug). Alternative repros that hit the same code path: vscode-pdf (open a PDF), `.ipynb` notebook renderers (open a notebook with rendered output), Live Preview.
3. Open in Safari (macOS or iPadOS) or any iOS browser. Open Web Inspector → Console.
4. Click the extension's activity-bar icon to open its webview panel.
5. Panel stays permanently blank. Console fires the cascade below.
6. Confirm it does NOT reproduce on V8: open the same URL in Chrome / Edge on macOS / Linux / Windows. Panel renders normally.

### Errors from the Dev Tools Console

```text
[Log] [Extension Host] Extension activated!
[Error] TypeError: Underlying ArrayBuffer has been detached from the view or out-of-bounds
  Uint8Array.prototype.slice
  VSBuffer.prototype.slice              (out/vs/base/common/buffer.js)
  ChunkStream._read                     (out/vs/base/parts/ipc/common/ipc.net.js)
  ProtocolReader.acceptChunk            (out/vs/base/parts/ipc/common/ipc.net.js)
  …
```

…fires once per IPC chunk on the management socket, then again on the extension-host socket. The cascade kills the channel; any subsequent IPC traffic for the extension is dead until reload.

---

## Root cause

`VSBuffer.prototype.slice` (`src/vs/base/common/buffer.ts`) currently returns:

```ts
public slice(start?: number, end?: number): VSBuffer {
    return new VSBuffer(this.buffer.subarray(start, end));
}
```

This is a *view* into the parent `ArrayBuffer`, not a copy — a deliberate perf optimization from #76076. The new `VSBuffer`'s `.buffer.buffer === this.buffer.buffer`.

In `vs/base/parts/ipc/common/ipc.net.ts`, `ChunkStream` queues incoming `VSBuffer` chunks and uses `.slice()` to extract messages:

```ts
private _read(byteCount: number, advance: boolean): VSBuffer {
    …
    if (this._chunks[0].byteLength > byteCount) {
        const result = this._chunks[0].slice(0, byteCount);         // ← view
        if (advance) {
            this._chunks[0] = this._chunks[0].slice(byteCount);     // ← view
            …
        }
        return result;
    }
    …
}
```

Both `result` and the residual chunk are views over the same `ArrayBuffer` that arrived in the WebSocket binary frame.

Downstream, the extracted message is delivered to the web-worker extension host iframe via `postMessage(msg, [transferList])`. The `transfer` list detaches the underlying `ArrayBuffer` in the originating window.

`ChunkStream` still holds chunks whose `.buffer` is now a view into a detached `ArrayBuffer`. The next read calls `Uint8Array.prototype.slice` on that view.

- **V8 (Chromium / Node / Electron)** is permissive about this. Historically `TypedArray.prototype.slice` on a view of a detached buffer returned an empty buffer; modern V8 throws, but the timing of the detach vs. the read is loose enough that the bug rarely fires in practice. This is why the bug is essentially invisible on every desktop browser and on Electron.
- **JavaScriptCore (Safari, iOS browsers)** throws synchronously and unconditionally per [TC39 ECMA-262 §25.1.2.1 (IsDetachedBuffer)](https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-isdetachedbuffer). The IPC reader unwinds, `handleUnexpectedError` fires, the channel is dead.

The detach-then-read race only fires for messages that need to be sliced (multi-chunk read) AND get transferred immediately downstream (webview content delivery). Routine editor traffic is single-chunk and short-lived, so it never hits the race. Webview-heavy extensions hit it on their very first frame.

---

## Candidate fixes (ranked by blast radius)

### A. Make `VSBuffer.slice` copy unconditionally (simplest, broadest)

```diff
 public slice(start?: number, end?: number): VSBuffer {
-    return new VSBuffer(this.buffer.subarray(start, end));
+    return new VSBuffer(this.buffer.slice(start, end));
 }
```

- **Pros**: one-character source change; semantics-correct in all environments; fully reversible; restores the pre-#76076 contract.
- **Cons**: walks back the perf optimization in all environments, including desktop Electron where the bug is invisible. Cost: one `memcpy` per `VSBuffer.slice` call. Sub-kilobyte typical IPC message sizes; in benchmarks against a sustained extension-host workload (Claude Code session, multiple notebook renderers) I haven't been able to measure the regression above noise, but I'm flagging it.

I'd open this as a **Draft RFC PR** alongside this issue. If maintainers prefer B or C below, easy to rewrite.

### B. Make `VSBuffer.slice` copy only in web/browser bundles (perf-preserving)

Use the existing `browser`/`common`/`node` source layout to split implementations:

```ts
// vs/base/common/buffer.ts (shared interface)
public slice(start?: number, end?: number): VSBuffer {
    return platform === 'browser'
        ? new VSBuffer(this.buffer.slice(start, end))
        : new VSBuffer(this.buffer.subarray(start, end));
}
```

(Or via a build-target conditional that swaps the implementation file.)

- **Pros**: zero perf impact on desktop Electron where the bug is invisible; only the affected platform pays the copy cost.
- **Cons**: introduces platform-conditional semantics for a primitive that should arguably behave the same everywhere. Asymmetric semantics is a footgun for future contributors.

### C. Copy at the IPC reader, not in `VSBuffer.slice` (most surgical)

Have `ChunkStream._read` produce owned buffers regardless of `slice` semantics:

```ts
// vs/base/parts/ipc/common/ipc.net.ts
private _read(byteCount: number, advance: boolean): VSBuffer {
    …
    if (this._chunks[0].byteLength > byteCount) {
        const view = this._chunks[0].slice(0, byteCount);
        const result = VSBuffer.alloc(view.byteLength);
        result.set(view, 0);                                    // copy
        if (advance) {
            const tail = this._chunks[0].slice(byteCount);
            const tailCopy = VSBuffer.alloc(tail.byteLength);
            tailCopy.set(tail, 0);
            this._chunks[0] = tailCopy;
            …
        }
        return result;
    }
    …
}
```

- **Pros**: minimum semantic blast radius; the rest of the codebase keeps the view-returning `slice` for everywhere else.
- **Cons**: more code; doesn't fix the same class of bug if it surfaces in any other code path that holds a view across a transferable boundary (and there are several such paths — anywhere a `VSBuffer` is held while another caller might post the original).

I believe (C) is partial: it cures the symptom in the IPC reader specifically, but the underlying contract violation (`slice` returns a view that can outlive its source's lifecycle) remains lurking elsewhere. Fine if maintainers want the minimum change; (A) is the more durable fix.

---

## Verifying the fix

A regression test that fails before (A) and passes after, suitable for `src/vs/base/test/common/buffer.test.ts`:

```ts
test('VSBuffer#slice returns an independent buffer that survives detachment of the source', () => {
    // VSBuffer instances are typically views into a larger ArrayBuffer
    // carrying IPC payload. Once a slice is extracted, downstream code may
    // transfer the original buffer to another execution context (e.g. an
    // extension-host iframe via postMessage). On WebKit (JavaScriptCore),
    // accessing any view into a detached ArrayBuffer throws synchronously
    // per ECMA-262 §25.1.2.1.
    //
    // VSBuffer#slice must therefore return a buffer that owns its data, not
    // a view into the source. This test exercises that contract by detaching
    // the source after slicing and asserting the slice is still readable.
    const source = VSBuffer.alloc(1024);
    source.buffer[0] = 42;
    const slice = source.slice(0, 512);

    // Detach the source's underlying ArrayBuffer.
    source.buffer.buffer.transfer();
    assert.strictEqual(source.buffer.byteLength, 0,
        'source.buffer should be detached');

    // The slice must still be readable — it owns its own storage.
    assert.strictEqual(slice.byteLength, 512);
    assert.strictEqual(slice.buffer[0], 42);
});
```

`ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer()` is in V8 since Chrome 114 (May 2023) and Node 21.7. If the runtime VS Code targets for unit tests is older, the test can use `structuredClone(buf, { transfer: [buf] })` or `MessageChannel.postMessage(_, [buf])` instead, with the same semantic effect.

---

## Production validation

We've been running fix (A) — applied as a one-character `sed` to the bundled `workbench.js` post-install — in production across three independent code-server deployments for ~24h. Before the patch, every webview-heavy extension session on Safari/iPad fired the cascade and stayed blank. After the patch, all WebKit clients work normally with no observable side effects or perf regressions. The same patch on V8 clients is a no-op (Chromium was already working).

Patch site in the minified bundle (offset confirmed at column ~97282 of line ~406 in `out/vs/code/browser/workbench/workbench.js` for code-server's vendored 1.107.x build):

```diff
-slice(i,e){return new a(this.buffer.subarray(i,e))}
+slice(i,e){return new a(this.buffer.slice(i,e))}
```

If maintainers want a self-contained Docker Compose reproducer (code-server + minimal extension + the broken-on-WebKit state vs. the patched-and-working state, with HAR captures), I'm happy to set one up.

---

## Why this matters

Every Safari and iOS user of browser-based VS Code is currently locked out of every webview-heavy extension. That includes:

- `vscode.dev` users on Safari (long surface, hard to deterministically repro outside of a binary-protocol extension).
- All `code-server` deployments — disproportionately the population that hits this, because code-server is browser-only and the iPad/iOS audience is large among self-hosted dev environments.
- `code serve-web` and Codespaces users on Safari/iOS.

Adjacent issues over the past several years that all roll up to the same root cause but were filed and closed without the underlying mechanism being identified:

- microsoft/vscode#213143 — "Safari 14 no longer able to load vscode.dev"
- coder/code-server#4433 — "Unable to load webview on iPad"
- coder/code-server#3410 — "Dynamically webview resources fail to load in some browser/OS combinations"

Downstream tracking: coder/code-server#7801. Draft PR for candidate fix (A) is open: #316842.

Draft PR for candidate fix (A) is up at #316842, opened as Draft to invite (B) / (C) feedback before un-drafting.

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version:  Works <= 1.89.1, broken >= 1.90.2
- OS Version: Linux x64 6.1.0-23-amd64

We develop an extension in which an existing tab gets replaced by a webview. We simply store a reference to the existing tab, open a webview and then close the original tab. This has worked fine up to version 1.89.1. On version >= 1.90.2 closing the tab using `vscode.window.tabGroups.close` fails with the exception `Tab close: Invalid tab not found!`. The tab has not been closed in the meantime nor did we get any close notifications for it via `vscode.window.tabGroups.onDidChangeTabs`. 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create an extension with the following function exposed as command: 
```typescript
async dummy(): Promise<void> {
	const tab = vscode.window.tabGroups.activeTabGroup.activeTab;
	if (!tab) {
		console.log("Please open a tab to test this code.");
		return;
	}

	const panel = vscode.window.createWebviewPanel(
		"TestPanel",
		"Test",
		tab.group.viewColumn);
	panel.webview.html = "<body>Hello World</body>";

	// Introduce a tiny delay
	await panel.webview.postMessage("hello");

	vscode.window.tabGroups.close(tab);
}
```
2. Open some tab and call the command 
3. Check the debug console for the  `Tab close: Invalid tab not found!` exception.

title: Chat terminal: show confirmation dialog and focus terminal when a password prompt is detected

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: accessibility
labels.description: Keyboard, mouse, ARIA, vision, screen readers (non-specific) issues

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
When the chat agent's `run_in_terminal` tool runs a command that prompts for a password (e.g., `sudo`, `ssh`, `gh auth login`, `npm publish` 2FA), the terminal silently waits for input. For users who rely on a screen reader — and even for sighted users not actively watching the terminal — there is no signal that input is required, and the chat session appears to hang.

### Proposal

Show an explicit confirmation dialog (or comparable UI affordance) the first time a running terminal command appears to be waiting for password / secret input. The dialog should:

- Announce via ARIA live region that the terminal is waiting for input.
- Offer a primary action that focuses the terminal so the user can type their secret directly (we should never round-trip the secret through the model).
- Offer a secondary action to cancel the command.

### Why

- **Accessibility**: screen-reader users currently get no announcement when a tool stalls on a hidden prompt.
- **Discoverability**: even sighted users can miss the prompt because the chat view is in front and the terminal isn't necessarily visible.
- **Safety**: routing the user straight to the terminal is the only correct way to enter a secret — `vscode_askQuestions` and similar would expose the value to the model.

### Notes

- Detection heuristic could reuse the same prompt-detection that the shell-integration / capabilities layer already does (`Password:`, `Passphrase:`, `[sudo] password for ...`, etc.).
- Should not appear for non-secret prompts — those are already covered by other UX.
