title: Add support for custom diff editors (proposed API)

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: custom-editors
labels.description: Custom editor API (webview based editors)

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
Plan for https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/138525

## Problem

Today, custom editor providers (`CustomEditorProvider`, `CustomTextEditorProvider`) can define how files are rendered and edited in a single editor. When a diff is opened for a file type handled by a custom editor, VS Code shows two side-by-side custom editor webviews wrapped in a standard `DiffEditorInput`. This works but has limitations:

- **No custom diff rendering** — the extension cannot provide a unified diff experience (e.g., overlaid image diffs, side-by-side binary comparisons with highlights, or structured document diffs)
- **Two separate webviews** — wasteful for cases where a single view could render a better diff
- **No access to both documents simultaneously** — each webview only sees one side

## Proposal

Extend the existing custom editor provider interfaces with an optional `resolveCustomDiffEditor` method. When present, VS Code opens a **single webview** for diffs, passing both documents to the extension. The extension renders the diff however it sees fit.

This reuses the existing `customEditors` contribution point and `CustomDocument` model — no new contribution point needed.

### Proposed API

```ts
// For CustomDocument-based providers
interface CustomReadonlyEditorProvider<T extends CustomDocument = CustomDocument> {
    // existing
    openCustomDocument(uri: Uri, openContext: CustomDocumentOpenContext, token: CancellationToken): Thenable<T> | T;
    resolveCustomEditor(document: T, webviewPanel: WebviewPanel, token: CancellationToken): Thenable<void> | void;

    // NEW — optional
    resolveCustomDiffEditor?(originalDocument: T, modifiedDocument: T, webviewPanel: WebviewPanel, token: CancellationToken): Thenable<void> | void;
}

// Editing support inherited from CustomEditorProvider<T> — save/revert/undo/redo apply to the modified document

// For TextDocument-based providers
interface CustomTextEditorProvider {
    // existing
    resolveCustomTextEditor(document: TextDocument, webviewPanel: WebviewPanel, token: CancellationToken): Thenable<void> | void;

    // NEW — optional
    resolveCustomTextDiffEditor?(originalDocument: TextDocument, modifiedDocument: TextDocument, webviewPanel: WebviewPanel, token: CancellationToken): Thenable<void> | void;
}
```

### How it works

1. Extension registers a `CustomEditorProvider` with `resolveCustomDiffEditor` implemented
2. User opens a diff for a file matching the editor's selector
3. Framework calls `openCustomDocument(uri)` for **both** the original and modified URIs — same function, same document model, ref-counted as usual via `CustomEditorModelManager`
4. Framework creates a single webview and calls `resolveCustomDiffEditor(originalDoc, modifiedDoc, webviewPanel)`
5. Extension renders a unified diff view in the webview
6. Save/revert/undo/redo on the modified document work through the existing `CustomDocument` infrastructure (`onDidChangeCustomDocument`, `saveCustomDocument`, etc.)
7. Extensions that do **not** implement `resolveCustomDiffEditor` fall back to the current behavior (two side-by-side custom editor webviews)

### Key design decisions

- **No new contribution point** — the existing `customEditors` selector already matches the file type for diffs
- **Reuses `openCustomDocument`** — both sides get documents from the same `openCustomDocument` call; if the document is already open in a regular editor, the ref-counted model is shared
- **Single webview** — the diff editor gets one `WebviewPanel`; the extension controls the entire diff rendering surface
- **Editing on modified side** — dirty state, save, revert, undo/redo all work through the existing `CustomDocument` model on the modified document; the original side is read-only by convention
- **Proposed API** — behind `customDiffEditorProvider` enablement flag

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Proposed API Surface

1. Create `src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.customDiffEditorProvider.d.ts` with the interfaces above
2. Regenerate `extensionsApiProposals.ts` (run `compile-api-proposal-names` gulp task)

### Phase 2: Protocol Layer

3. Extend `ExtHostCustomEditorsShape` in `extHost.protocol.ts` — add:
   ```ts
   $resolveCustomDiffEditor(
       originalResource: UriComponents,
       modifiedResource: UriComponents,
       newWebviewHandle: WebviewHandle,
       viewType: string,
       initData: { title: string; contentOptions: IWebviewContentOptions; options: IWebviewPanelOptions; active: boolean },
       position: EditorGroupColumn,
       cancellation: CancellationToken
   ): Promise<void>;
   ```
4. Extend `$registerCustomEditorProvider` / `$registerTextEditorProvider` to pass `supportsDiffEditor: boolean` via capabilities
5. Implement `$resolveCustomDiffEditor` in `extHostCustomEditors.ts` — create one webview panel, retrieve both documents, call the provider's `resolveCustomDiffEditor`
6. Detect diff-capable providers during registration — check for `resolveCustomDiffEditor` method on provider, pass `supportsDiffEditor` flag to main thread

### Phase 3: Custom Diff Editor Input & Pane

Following the `NotebookDiffEditorInput` pattern:

7. Create `CustomDiffEditorInput` in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/customEditor/browser/customDiffEditorInput.ts`
   - Extends `DiffEditorInput`
   - Holds `original: CustomEditorInput`, `modified: CustomEditorInput`, plus a single `IOverlayWebview`
   - `editorId` returns `this.modified.viewType` (routes to the correct pane)
   - `resolve()` acquires models for both sides, resolves the webview
8. Create `CustomDiffEditor` pane in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/customEditor/browser/customDiffEditor.ts`
   - Extends `EditorPane`, renders the single webview
   - `setInput()` calls `input.resolve()`, claims the webview, manages layout
9. Register pane in `customEditor.contribution.ts`:
   - `EditorPaneDescriptor.create(CustomDiffEditor, ...)` bound to `CustomDiffEditorInput`
   - Register serializer for `CustomDiffEditorInput`

### Phase 4: Main Thread Wiring

10. Extend `CustomEditorCapabilities` in `customEditor.ts` with `supportsDiffEditor?: boolean`
11. Modify `createDiffEditorInput` factory in `customEditors.ts`:
    - If `supportsDiffEditor` is true → create `CustomDiffEditorInput` (single webview)
    - If false → existing behavior (two `CustomEditorInput`s in standard `DiffEditorInput`)
12. Extend `registerEditorProvider` in `mainThreadCustomEditors.ts`:
    - Register a webview resolver matching `CustomDiffEditorInput`
    - `resolveWebview`: create models for both sides, call `$resolveCustomDiffEditor` on ext host

### Phase 5: API Gate

13. In `extHost.api.impl.ts`, call `checkProposedApiEnabled(extension, 'customDiffEditorProvider')` when a diff-capable provider is detected

---

## Files to change

| File | Action |
|------|--------|

title: Feature Request: Set an environment variable (e.g. COPILOT_AGENT) when agent/subagent runs a terminal command

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
## Problem

When VS Code Copilot Chat (or its subagents) executes a command in the terminal via tools like `run_in_terminal` or `execution_subagent`, there is **no way for CLI tools to detect** that the command was initiated by an AI agent rather than a human user.

Other AI coding agents already set environment variables in spawned subprocesses:

| Agent | Environment Variable(s) |
|-------|------------------------|
| Claude Code CLI | `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE` |
| Cursor | `CURSOR_AGENT` |
| Codex | `CODEX_SANDBOX`, `CODEX_THREAD_ID` |
| Gemini CLI | `GEMINI_CLI` |
| Augment | `AUGMENT_AGENT` |
| Opencode | `OPENCODE_CLIENT`, `OPENCODE` |
| AMP | `AMP_CURRENT_THREAD_ID` |
| Antigravity | `ANTIGRAVITY_AGENT` |

There is also a generic convention (`AI_AGENT`) used by tools like [`shipfastlabs/agent-detector`](https://github.com/shipfastlabs/agent-detector) and discussed in [agentsmd/agents.md#136](https://github.com/agentsmd/agents.md/issues/136).

## Proposal

When Copilot Chat (or a subagent) runs a command in the terminal, set an environment variable such as:

```
COPILOT_AGENT=1
```

or follow the generic convention:

```
AI_AGENT=copilot
```

This would allow CLI tools to:
- **Output machine-readable formats** (JSON) instead of ANSI-decorated human-readable text, reducing token usage and improving parsing accuracy
- **Disable interactive prompts** that would otherwise block the agent
- **Skip animations and progress bars** that add noise to agent output

## Context

The VS Code core team closed [microsoft/vscode#258911](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/258911) arguing that agent terminals should behave like user terminals. However, this is a different request — it is not about changing terminal behavior but about **giving programs the ability to optimize their output format** when they know an agent is consuming it. The terminal itself stays the same; the program running inside it adapts.

This is analogous to how `CI=true` allows programs to detect CI/CD environments and adjust behavior accordingly (disable interactive prompts, simplify output, etc.). The same pattern is valuable for AI agent environments.

## Real-world use case

The [Bootgly PHP Framework](https://github.com/bootgly/bootgly) test runner (`bootgly test`) detects AI agents via environment variables and outputs compact JSON instead of ANSI-formatted text when an agent is detected. This dramatically improves the agent's ability to parse test results accurately. However, Copilot Chat currently cannot be detected because it does not set any identifying environment variable when running terminal commands.

title: large MCP outputs blocked in sandbox
body:
Type: Bug Chat sanbox mode is on: 1. Copilot Chat Sonnet 4.6 prompt that triggers MCP server use and generates a large output (in my case, Metabase MCP) 2. The large output is written to `~/.vscode-server/data/User/workspaceStorage//GitHub.copilot-chat/chat-session-resources///content.json` 3. Copilot attempts to run parsing commands such as jq on the `content.json` 4. Command fails to run inside sandbox with file not found 5. Copilot asks for approval to run outside sandbox What I want: At step 4, Copilot running in sandbox should be able to access `content.json` and perform typical parsing and filtering without needing approval. I tried adding "/home/mra/.vscode-server" read access to the sandbox, but then the agent got more permission errors, and it prompted for approval again. The agent says a work around is to use read_file, but I think it wants access to tools like jq etc. Extension version: 0.48.1 VS Code version: Code 1.120.0 (0958016b2af9f09bb4257e0df4a95e2f90590f9f, 2026-05-12T20:17:22Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2

title: Deadlock in extension host activation blocks tunnels auth

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: important
labels.description: Issue identified as high-priority

labels.name: extension-host
labels.description: Extension host issues

milestone.title: On Deck
milestone.description: The Issues assigned to the **On Deck** milestone under consideration for the upcoming milestones.

body:
1. Try to connect to a remote tunnel from vscode desktop
2. You get stuck on "connecting to xxx..." 

(for some reason this works on a couple of my devices, but not all, I have not investigated why)

The sequence is:

1. Remote tunnels needs auth. It requests an auth provider. This ends up activating auth extensions by event using `ActivationKind.Immediate`, which we added to signal that we need to activate these extensions without waiting for extensions in general to be ready (which only happens when a remote is connected)

1. This signals all ready or local extension hosts to start activating.

	https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/2e3f347c80edcd93738b39071ef9fa93394c7656/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/abstractExtensionService.ts#L1023-L1042

1. The webworker extension hosts wait for its proxy to be ready.

	https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/2e3f347c80edcd93738b39071ef9fa93394c7656/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionHostManager.ts#L342

1. The webworker extension host gets init data as part of `_performHandshake` while creating its proxy.

	https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/2e3f347c80edcd93738b39071ef9fa93394c7656/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/browser/webWorkerExtensionHost.ts#L293-L294

1. This ends up calling `_getExtensionRegistrySnapshotWhenReady` which deadlocks because it waits on `_installedExtensionsReady`, which does not happen until there is a remote.

	https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/2e3f347c80edcd93738b39071ef9fa93394c7656/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/abstractExtensionService.ts#L1074-L1076


This changed in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/8f0fd7e348a467ded5f481d0edbe440ec96b95d6, but I think it also was not working before that, not super clear. The local process extension service uses the scanner to find extensions that will end up getting run there, maybe the webworker extension host should be aligned similarly somehow?

I have node and npm setup using Volta. Using gpt5.5 and auto pilot, I'm seeing it get hung up whenever it runs a npm command with the following error:

matb@mjbmbp2 vscode %  ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 PATH="$PATH:/Applications/Visual Studio Code - Insiders.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/@vscode/ripgrep/bin" TMPDIR="/Users/matb/.vscode-insiders/tmp/tmp_vscode_1" CLAUDE_TMPDIR="/Users/matb/.vscode-insiders/tmp/tmp_vscode_1" "/Applications/Visual Studio Code - Insiders.app/Contents/MacOS/Code - Insiders" "/Applications/Visual Studio Code - Insiders.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime/dist/cli.js" --settings "/Users/matb/.vscode-insiders/tmp/tmp_vscode_1/vscode-sandbox-settings-9c51b1ca-d000-47cc-a135-6bf59c3978f6.json" -c 'npm run gulp compile-extension:markdown-language-features'

This happens for every single npm command it runs and I think is resulting in longer runtimes and extra usage since it's continually trying to workaround these failures

title: Snippet Choice Traps Focus

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: **Yes**

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.96.0
- OS Version: Windows 11, and Mac OS 

```
Version: 1.96.0 (user setup)
Commit: 138f619c86f1199955d53b4166bef66ef252935c
Date: 2024-12-11T02:29:09.626Z
Electron: 32.2.6
ElectronBuildId: 10629634
Chromium: 128.0.6613.186
Node.js: 20.18.1
V8: 12.8.374.38-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22631
```

My extension's Language Server provides snippets to complete function documentation as part of its `textDocument/completion` handler. Here is an example snippet it provides:

```
;---------
; SCOPE:        ${1|PRIVATE,INTERNAL,PUBLIC|}
; DESCRIPTION:  ${2}
; PARAMETERS:
;   subject (${3|I,O,IO|},${4|REQ,OPT|}${5}) - ${6}
;   body (${7|I,O,IO|},${8|REQ,OPT|}${9}) - ${10}
;   inst (${11|I,O,IO|},${12|REQ,OPT|}${13}) - ${14}
;   interval (${15|I,O,IO|},${16|REQ,OPT|}${17}) - ${18}
;${0}
;---------
```

When the user offers the snippet, the content is inserted correctly. However, when they tab to one of the lines below `PARAMETERS:` and stop on any of the `${X|I,O,IO|}` and `${X|REQ,OPT|}` tab choices, they can no longer Shift+Tab to a tab stop before the choice. Interestingly, if the user has reached the `${2}` tab stop, they can tab back to the `$1` choice.  

In order to verify this was not an issue with the extension itself, I copied this snippet to be a user-configured global snippet per [these instructions](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editor/userdefinedsnippets#_create-your-own-snippets).

```json
{
	"Function Header": {
		"scope": "",
		"prefix": ";",
		"body": [
			";---------",
			"; SCOPE:        ${1|PRIVATE,INTERNAL,PUBLIC|}",
			"; DESCRIPTION:  ${2}",
			"; PARAMETERS:",
			";   subject (${3|I,O,IO|},${4|REQ,OPT|}${5}) - ${6}",
			";   body (${7|I,O,IO|},${8|REQ,OPT|}${9}) - ${10}",
			";   inst (${11|I,O,IO|},${12|REQ,OPT|}${13}) - ${14}",
			";   interval (${15|I,O,IO|},${16|REQ,OPT|}${17}) - ${18}",
			";${0}",
			";---------"
		]
	}
}
```

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create this snippet in your global snippet configuration
2. In a new document, accept the snippet
3. Tab to the `I/O/IO` tab choice on the line `subject` parameter.
4. You can no longer shift tab back to `${2}`

title: On Windows, the integrated PowerShell terminal gets a PATH with broken vscode CLI when started using Code.exe

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: install-update
labels.description: VS Code installation and upgrade system issues

labels.name: windows
labels.description: VS Code on Windows issues

body:
Type: Bug Observed behavior: - If VS Code is fully closed and then launched from the Start menu (or directly via Code.exe), the integrated PowerShell terminal resolves `code` to `Code.exe` instead of `bin\code.cmd`. - In this state, commands like `code --version` open a new VS Code window instead of behaving like the CLI. - If VS Code is fully closed and then launched from `code.cmd` from a terminal, the integrated PowerShell terminal PATH is correct and `code` resolves to `...\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd`. - After launching once via `code.cmd`, opening additional VS Code windows keeps the good behavior. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Fully exit all VS Code windows/processes. 2. Launch VS Code from Start menu (or directly from `C:\Users\\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe`). 3. Open a new integrated PowerShell terminal. 4. Run: `Get-Command code -All` 5. Observe that `code` resolves to `...\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` before `...\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd`. 6. Optionally run `code --version` and observe that it opens a new VS Code window instead of printing version info. Control case: 1. Fully exit all VS Code windows/processes. 2. Launch VS Code from terminal via `code.cmd` / `code .`. 3. Open a new integrated PowerShell terminal. 4. Run: `Get-Command code -All` 5. Observe that `code` resolves correctly to `...\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd`. Expected: The integrated terminal should get consistent PATH / command resolution regardless of whether VS Code was launched from Start menu / Code.exe or from `code.cmd`. Actual: The integrated terminal inherits or constructs a different PATH depending on launch method, causing `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` in one case and `code.cmd` in the other. VS Code version: Code 1.111.0 (ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb, 2026-03-06T23:06:10Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Open Agents Window keybinding conflicts with screen reader shortcuts

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: accessibility
labels.description: Keyboard, mouse, ARIA, vision, screen readers (non-specific) issues

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
The default keybinding for `workbench.action.openAgentsWindow` is `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+A`, which conflicts with many screen reader shortcuts (e.g. NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver pass-through and selection chords).

## Fix

When `accessibilityModeEnabled` is true, register the keybinding as `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Alt+A` instead of `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+A`. Document the screen-reader variant in the Chat Accessibility Help dialog so users discover it.

Fix in branch `merogge/agents-window-kb-screenreader`.

/label ~accessibility ~chat

title: Only surface "sort imports" and "remove unused imports" when TS client is running/useTsgo is not active

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
See also https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/issues/1724

We want the TypeScript native preview extension to contribute commands for "sort imports" and "remove unused imports". Unfortunately, even when the `js/ts...useTsgo` setting is on, the built-in extension still makes the two commands available.

Ideally, we would have our extension register the same command, but double-registering a command name causes errors at runtime. So instead, each extension needs to defer to the other.

title: localhost is always blocked when chat.agent.networkFilter is enabled

labels.name: chat-ext-issue
labels.description: Triaging assistance tag. Automatically applied if an issue comes from the chat oss repo.

body:
- VS Code Version: 1.118.1
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

**Description**
When `chat.agent.networkFilter` is set to `true`, the domain `localhost` is always blocked, even if it is explicitly listed in `chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains`. It seems the filter ignores the whitelist specifically for local addresses.

**Configuration**
```json
"chat.agent.networkFilter": true,
"chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains": [
    "localhost",
    "127.0.0.1",
    "[::1]",
    "*.github.com"
],
"chat.agent.deniedNetworkDomains": [
    "*.my-internal.network.domain"
]
```

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Enable the network filter: `"chat.agent.networkFilter": true`.
2. Add `localhost` and `*.github.com` to the allowed domains list.
3. Prompt Copilot: "Open integrated browser on localhost".
4. Result: Access is denied with a network policy error.
5. Prompt Copilot: "Open integrated browser on github.com".
6. Result: Works correctly.

**Expected behavior**
`localhost` should be respected in the `allowedNetworkDomains` list.

**Actual behavior**
`localhost` remains always blocked as long as the network filter is active, regardless of any whitelist entries. If I set `chat.agent.networkFilter` to `false`, it works again.

title: Chronicle: sessions.summary contains raw JSON instead of human-readable text

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

`sessions.summary` often contains raw JSON blobs like `[{"type":"tool_result",…}]` instead of human-readable text. This makes `WHERE summary LIKE '%keyword%'` queries unreliable and confuses the Chronicle intent LLM.

## Current behavior

`_backfillFromSpanAttributes` sets `summary` from `CopilotChatAttr.USER_REQUEST`, truncated to 100 chars. Additionally, `_handleAgentSpan` sets summary from the first user message if no summary exists yet.

The issue is that `USER_REQUEST` or the first user message event content can itself be a raw JSON blob (e.g. tool results, structured messages), which gets stored as-is.

## Expected behavior

`summary` should be a short human-readable description of the session. If the first user message is structured/JSON, it should either:
- Be skipped in favor of a later plain-text message, or
- Have the text content extracted from the JSON structure

## Files

- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/chronicle/vscode-node/sessionStoreTracker.ts` — `_backfillFromSpanAttributes` and `_handleAgentSpan`
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/intents/node/chronicleIntent.ts` — schema warns "summary may contain raw JSON"

Follow-up from #312292

title: Chronicle: populate agent_name on sessions from invoke_agent spans

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

`sessions.agent_name` is always empty in the local Chronicle store. The code in `_initSession` reads `GenAiAttr.AGENT_NAME` (`gen_ai.agent.name`) from the span, but this attribute is only set on **inference spans** (in `chatMLFetcher.ts` line 250), not on `invoke_agent` spans that trigger session creation.

## Current code

```typescript
// sessionStoreTracker.ts _initSession
const sessionSource = (span.attributes[GenAiAttr.AGENT_NAME] as string | undefined) ?? 'unknown';
this._bufferSessionUpsert({ id: sessionId, host_type: 'vscode', agent_name: sessionSource });
```

This always falls through to `'unknown'` because `invoke_agent` spans don't carry `gen_ai.agent.name`.

## Expected behavior

`agent_name` should identify the agent/mode that created the session (e.g. `'copilot'`, `'agent'`, `'edit'`, extension-contributed agents). The value is useful for the Chronicle intent to distinguish session types.

## Suggested approach

1. Check what attributes on `invoke_agent` spans carry the agent/mode name — likely `copilot_chat.agent_slug` or similar.
2. Update `_initSession` to read the correct attribute.
3. If no single attribute works, consider backfilling `agent_name` from child spans in `_backfillFromSpanAttributes`.
4. Add a test case to `sessionStoreTracker` or the common test suite.

## Files

- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/chronicle/vscode-node/sessionStoreTracker.ts` — `_initSession` method
- `extensions/copilot/src/platform/otel/common/genAiAttributes.ts` — attribute constants
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompt/node/chatMLFetcher.ts` line 250 — where `AGENT_NAME` IS set (on inference spans)

Follow-up from #312292

title: Expand terminal tool output compression: more commands + subcommand parsing

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
Follow-up to #315376, which landed the initial `IToolResultCompressor` service with filters for `git diff`/`git show`, `ls -l`, and `npm install`/`yarn`/`pnpm install`. The framework is in place but we're leaving lots of token savings on the table — the matchers are coarse, only a handful of commands are covered, we don't look inside compound or pipelined invocations, and we re-emit the same output every time the model re-runs a read-only command.

This issue tracks bringing our compression closer to parity with [`codejunkie99/ztk`](https://github.com/codejunkie99/ztk) (the proxy that inspired #315376 — note their "never make it worse" rule is already adopted) while keeping everything in-process so we don't need a sidecar binary or hook adapter.

## Goals

1. **Cover more high-volume commands.** Expand the filter set toward ztk's category coverage (git, test runners, file ops, build tools, linters, infrastructure, utilities).
2. **Parse subcommands and flags properly.** Today the matchers are essentially `startsWith` checks on the command string. Extend to a lightweight argv-aware parser so we respect subcommands and flags (`git log` vs `git diff`, `npm install` vs `npm run build`, `docker ps` vs `docker build`).
3. **Handle command composition.** Detect commands embedded in pipelines, `&&`/`||`/`;` chains, env-prefixed forms (`FOO=bar git diff`), and `sudo`/`time`/`xargs`-prefixed forms so the right filter still fires.
4. **Add session memory / response dedup.** When the agent re-runs a read-only command and nothing has changed since last time, reply with a one-line "same as last run" instead of the full output again. Mutation commands invalidate related cached entries.
5. **Guarantee no-touch for structured data and errors.** Make the existing "never make it worse" rule explicit at the registry level so JSON / YAML / TOML / error streams / exit-code-carrying lines never get rewritten.

## Candidate commands to add

Mirrors ztk's built-in filter set, scoped to what `run_in_terminal` actually invokes:

- **VCS**: `git status`, `git log`, `git add`, `git commit`, `git push`, `git blame`, `git stash list` (already covered: `git diff`/`git show`).
- **Test runners**: `cargo test`/`cargo nextest`, `pytest`, `go test`, `npm test`, `pnpm test`, `yarn test`, `jest`/`vitest` (including `npx jest`/`npx vitest`), `playwright`. Pattern: keep failure block + summary line, drop dots / `PASSED`-only lines.
- **Build tools**: `cargo build`/`cargo check` (drop `Compiling …` progress, keep diagnostics), `go build`, `tsc`, `zig build`, `make` (drop `Entering/Leaving directory`), `gradle`/`mvn` (drop `Download …` chatter, keep BUILD result + errors), `dotnet build`.
- **Linters**: `eslint` (drop clean files, keep findings), `ruff`, `mypy`, `clippy`, `golangci-lint`, `prettier`, `rubocop`.
- **File ops**: `cat` (large files → signatures/declarations only — see below), `find` (cap + summarize on many hits), `grep`/`rg` (cap + summarize), `wc`, `head`, `tail`, `tree` (depth cap), `du -h` (collapse deep trees). (already covered: `ls -l`/`ls -la`.)
- **Infrastructure**: `docker ps`/`docker images` (column trim), `docker build` (drop intermediate layer chatter, keep `Step N/M` + errors), `docker compose`, `kubectl get`/`kubectl describe` (column trim), `curl -v` (collapse handshake noise), `env`/`printenv` (alpha-sort + dedupe).
- **Package managers**: `npm ls`/`npm list`/`npm outdated`, `pnpm list`, `yarn list`, `pip list`, `brew install`/`brew update`, `apt-get install` (progress drop). (already covered: `npm install`/`yarn`/`pnpm install`.)
- **Utilities**: GitHub CLI (`gh`, especially `gh checks`), Python tracebacks (dedupe repeated frames), log files (deduplicate consecutive identical lines with a `(×N)` suffix).

## Subcommand and flag parsing

Instead of:

```ts
if (cmd.startsWith('git diff') || cmd.startsWith('git show')) { … }
```

introduce a small argv tokenizer that returns `{ program, subcommands: string[], flags: Map<string, string|true>, positionals: string[] }` and let filters register against `(program, subcommands[0])` pairs with optional flag predicates. This:

- avoids false positives (e.g. `git difftool` accidentally matching the `git diff` filter today),
- lets filters opt in to specific flag combinations (e.g. `ls` only when `-l` is present),
- makes it easy to bail out for unknown subcommands rather than mis-compressing.

## Composition handling

Walk the command line to find the relevant program(s):

- skip leading env assignments (`FOO=bar BAZ=qux …`),
- skip wrappers: `sudo`, `time`, `nice`, `env`, `xargs`, `stdbuf`,
- for pipelines (`a | b | c`) and `&&`/`||`/`;` separators, apply filters to each segment independently and recombine,
- preserve segment boundaries in the output so the model still sees `---` between the pieces of a chained command.

A regex-only approach won't handle quoting correctly here; a tiny POSIX-ish tokenizer (split on unquoted separators, respect single/double quotes and backslash escapes) is enough — we don't need a real shell parser.

## Session memory (response dedup)

Cache the *compressed* output of read-only commands keyed by `(cwd, full command line)` with per-command-class TTLs, modeled on ztk:

| Class | Example commands | TTL |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fast-changing | `git status`, `ls` | 30s |
| Medium | test runners (`npm test`, `pytest`, `cargo test`) | 2m |
| Slow-changing | `git log`, `find`, `tree` | 5m |

On cache hit, return `Same output as last run (<timestamp>).` instead of re-emitting bytes.

Mutation commands invalidate related cached entries — e.g. `git add`/`git commit` clears all `git status`/`git diff` entries for that cwd, `npm install` clears `npm ls` entries, file writes through the editor clear `ls`/`find`/`tree` for the affected directory.

Telemetry: add a `cacheHit: boolean` field to `toolResultCompressed` so we can quantify the dedup savings separately from filter savings.

## Hard "do not touch" list

Make these guarantees explicit at the registry level so a misbehaving filter can't violate them:

- Stderr / non-zero-exit segments pass through unchanged ("you need errors").
- Exit codes always preserved (we already surface them; ensure no filter rewrites the trailing exit indicator).
- Outputs under 80 bytes skipped (already implemented in #315376).
- Outputs that parse as JSON / YAML / TOML at the top level skipped — no comment stripping or reformatting.

## Acceptance

- [ ] New `run_in_terminal` filters land for at least the VCS, test-runner, and build-tool buckets above.
- [ ] Filter registry keys on `(program, subcommand)` with optional flag predicates; regression test that `git difftool` does **not** match the `git diff` filter.
- [ ] Compound commands (pipeline, `&&`, env prefix, `sudo`/`time` wrappers) route each segment to the right filter; covered by unit tests.
- [ ] Session-memory cache implemented with the three TTL classes and mutation invalidation; `toolResultCompressed` gains a `cacheHit` field.
- [ ] Structured-data / stderr / exit-code guard rails enforced at the registry level (filter throwing or violating the guarantee → original output preserved, per the "never make it worse" rule from #315376).
- [ ] Telemetry continues to report `{ toolName, filters, beforeBytes, afterBytes }` so we can quantify per-command savings; new `cacheHit` field reports dedup savings.

## Out of scope

- Compressing output from non-terminal tools — the existing `IToolResultCompressor` is generic but this issue is scoped to `run_in_terminal`.
- Lossy summarization via LLM. Filters stay deterministic and cheap.
- Configurable per-user filter lists. Setting remains the existing single `chat.tools.compressOutput.enabled` toggle.
- Shipping as a standalone proxy/binary. Everything stays inside the extension host.

## References

- #315376 — initial compressor service and three filters.
- [codejunkie99/ztk](https://github.com/codejunkie99/ztk) — the external proxy that inspired #315376; this issue ports more of its design (filter coverage, session memory, structured-data guard rails) into the in-process compressor.

title: run_in_terminal: promote sync command to background after N seconds of no output

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
The `run_in_terminal` tool can block a chat turn indefinitely when a synchronous command produces no output for an extended period. Today the foreground (sync) wait races the following: command completion, "continue in background", input-needed detection, and an *optional* timeout that only fires when the model passed a `timeout` parameter (or when `chat.tools.terminal.enforceTimeoutFromModel` is true *and* the model passed one). If the model omits `timeout` — which it usually does for "quick" commands — the foreground call has no escape hatch and the turn stalls until the user intervenes.

## Real repro

A chat session in `~/Developer/firefox-main-test` ran:

```
jj config set --user user.name "…" \
  && jj config set --user user.email "…" \
  && cat ~/.config/jj/config.toml
```

`jj` snapshots the working copy on *every* invocation, including `jj config set --user`, which doesn't touch the repo. In a Firefox-sized checkout the snapshot takes several minutes. The agent saw zero output and waited 7 m 43 s before the shell finally interrupted. During that window the model could not distinguish "still running" from "hung", and could not bail out — there was no signal between "command started" and "command returned".

This is the general shape, not a `jj` quirk: any silent command (a slow socket, a stalled SSH handshake, a `git fetch` against a dead remote, …) hits the same wall.

## Proposed fix

Add an **idle-output promotion** to the foreground race in `runInTerminalTool.ts`:

- Subscribe to `toolTerminal.instance.onData` and (re)start a timer on each chunk.
- If the timer fires without the command completing, win the race with a new `{ type: 'idleSilence' }` result — same handling as the existing `timeout` branch: mark the execution background, return the terminal ID + output collected so far, and append a steering hint.
- Gate on a new setting `chat.tools.terminal.idleSilenceTimeoutMs` (default `60_000`; set to `0` to disable).

Crucially this **does not kill the process**. It just hands control back to the model with a real signal so it can call `get_terminal_output` to keep watching, `send_to_terminal` if it suspects an input prompt, or `kill_terminal` if it judges the command stuck. The shell keeps running until the model or the user decides otherwise.

Why this is safe for the noisy-but-slow commands we care about (`npm install`, `cargo build`, big test runs, watch tasks): they emit output regularly, so the timer resets and they never trip the threshold. The cases this catches are exactly the silent ones today's foreground path has no answer for.

## Acceptance

- [ ] New setting `chat.tools.terminal.idleSilenceTimeoutMs` (default 60_000, 0 disables).
- [ ] Foreground race in `runInTerminalTool.ts` adds an `idleSilence` candidate that fires after N ms of no `onData` activity.
- [ ] On idle-silence win: promote to background (don't kill), return the terminal ID + output collected so far, append a steering note explaining the silence.
- [ ] Listener is disposed via the outer `DisposableStore` so we don't leak handlers when another race candidate wins.
- [ ] Async (`waitStrategy === 'idle'`) path is unchanged — it already has the OutputMonitor idle signal.
- [ ] Telemetry captures the new exit reason (`error = 'idleSilence'`).

## Related

- The compression follow-up (#315881) and the broader terminal/chat backlog this is part of.
- [#314081](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/314081) — once stop reliably SIGINTs the foreground process, the model's `kill_terminal` response to an idle-silence promotion will be clean end-to-end.

Type: Bug 1. Open a powershell terminal inside vscode terminal 2. Run `code --list-extensions` or `code --install-extension "ms-vscode.cpptools"` 3. Code UI opens but the powershell command do not return nothing Outside VSCode, in a powershell terminal, the command works well. My problem is that I use a task to check and install the extensions from extensions.json. This is the code of the task: ```json { "label": "Install All Recommended Extensions", "type": "shell", "windows": { "command": "foreach ($ext in (Get-Content -Raw .vscode/extensions.json | ConvertFrom-Json).recommendations) { Write-Host Installing $ext; code --install-extension $ext; }" }, "linux": { // This needs jq to be installed, you must run: sudo apt install jq "command": "cat .vscode/extensions.json | jq .recommendations[] | xargs -n 1 code . --install-extension" }, "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }, "presentation": { "reveal": "silent", "close": true }, "problemMatcher": [] }, ``` This only happens in Windows, on WSL this does not fail. title: Brackets Snippet deleting chars

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

body:
Brackets Snippet in replace mode deletes char when prefix already exists
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70acd3cb-be83-486f-a9bd-9acd809b45c4)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Set `editor.suggest.insertMode` to `replace`
1. Create new global snippet `workbench.action.openSnippets`
```jsonc
{
	"Brackets": {
		"scope": "plaintext",
		"prefix": "()",
		"body": "()",
		"description": "Set `editor.suggest.insertMode` to `replace`\nWhen `()` already exists, the snippet deletes the next following char\n "
	}
}
```
2. Create new `plaintext` file with example text
```
()!!
()  
()aa
```
3. Place cursor in-between brackets
4. Press ctrl+space and select `Brackets` Snippet

Expected:
nothing
(Cursor moves 1 to the right)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b1c21c8-0ecc-4593-9c10-505f995e9880)

Actual:
1 character is deleted after the `()` 🐛
doesn't matter if the char is a `wordPattern` or `wordSeparator` or `whitespace`
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26548e46-133c-4f66-94df-4d06c23c6579)



Notice that using other characters other than brackets `()` does **not** result in chars being deleted
